data.body:
=== RESEARCH REPORT ===
=== research: LLM-planning failure modes (deeper) + verdict-composition A/B/C
=== agent a7c4d9b640ee08c32

All four research threads are complete and cross-corroborated by my own independent primary-source verifications. I now have everything needed to synthesize the final deliverable.

One important reconciliation I'll flag prominently: there is a terminology subtlety in the Kambhampati FP figure that both my fetch and the verdict-composition agent surfaced, and it actually *strengthens* (not weakens) the case against gating on LLM judgment. Here is the consolidated report.

---

# Plan-review gate: research shoring-up — findings

Four research threads, cross-checked against my own fetches of the primary papers. Citations are arXiv IDs; contested/dated claims are flagged inline. The most load-bearing claims (Kambhampati self-verification, MAST, Plan-and-Solve, LLM-Modulo, TravelPlanner, self-verification-limits) I verified independently against the source abstracts/full-text, not just via subagents.

---

## 1. Coverage gap: failure modes our base+overlay dimensions do NOT catch

I mapped the established taxonomies (MAST 2503.13657 — all 14 modes confirmed, κ=0.88; Plan-and-Solve 2305.04091; TravelPlanner 2402.01622 — GPT-4 final pass 0.6% confirmed; PlanBench 2206.10498; survey 2402.02716) plus newer 2024-2026 coding-agent/SWE-bench error analyses against B0-B4, C1-C2, L1-L4, O1-O6.

**Prioritized uncovered modes (frequent AND detectable from a static read of plan + codebase) → the check each needs:**

| # | Uncovered failure mode | Frequency / evidence | Reviewable check to add | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **U1** | **Nonexistent package / dependency ("slopsquatting")** — plan imports a package that doesn't exist (supply-chain attack surface) | **19.7%** of recommended packages don't exist; 43% recur across reruns — **2406.10279** (USENIX Sec 2025) | Extend B1 to check planned imports against the **registry + repo lockfile/manifest**, not just in-repo symbols. B1 currently resolves in-repo paths/symbols only. | HIGH — strongest single finding in the corpus |
| **U2** | **Fault-localization failure** — plan targets the wrong file/function for the change | #1 SWE-bench failure source: ~20-41% file-level, 72-97% function-level miss — **2310.06770, 2509.13941, 2407.01489** | New leaf check: does the plan's named target location plausibly contain the code the criterion implies? (cross-ref criterion → codebase). Not in any current dim. | HIGH (dominance), MEDIUM (rates scaffold-specific) |
| **U3** | **Test-gaming / test-deletion / reward-hacking intent** — plan proposes to pass the test by hardcoding outputs, test-mode branches, or weakening/deleting the test | Cross-lab (GPT-5 card); weak SWE-bench tests inflate scores ~6-7pp (**2410.06992** SWE-Bench+, **2506.09289** UTBoost) | New check: scan plan strategy for test-keyed special-casing / proposals to modify the test that defines the AC. **L3 covers RED→GREEN presence but NOT gaming.** Pair with write-protected verification. | MEDIUM (rates), HIGH (plan-detectability) |
| **U4** | **Irreversible/destructive action without checkpoint** — drop table, rm -rf, force-push, mass-delete, send/transfer, with no backup/rollback preceding | ToolEmu best agent failed 23.9%; SafeAgentBench rejected only 10% of hazardous tasks — **2309.15817, 2412.13178** | **O4 triggers on migration/rename keywords** but the evidence shows keyword-triggering misses destructive ops described without trigger words. Add the cheap always-on "destructive/irreversible?" sniff the design doc already floats, against an **irreversible-action allowlist**. | MEDIUM (emulated envs) |
| **U5** | **Missing verification / termination criteria** — no checkpoint after a risky step, or no explicit "done" condition | MAST: no/incomplete verification 8.2%, incorrect 9.1%, unaware-of-termination 12.4% (~21-23% of failures) — **2503.13657** | New structural check: does each risky step have a following verify/checkpoint, and does the plan have a stop criterion? **L3 is TDD-shaped; this is broader (any risky step, not just code).** | HIGH (MAST corpus) |
| **U6** | **Self-review as the verification mechanism** — plan's verification is "agent reviews own work" rather than an external/sound check | Self-critique *collapses* planning performance; sound external verifier recovers it — **2310.01798, 2402.08115** (both independently verified) | New check on the plan's verification *architecture*: flag plans whose only verification is self-review vs running tests/type-check/sound oracle. Not in current dims. | HIGH but nuanced (intrinsic self-correction fails; external feedback helps) |
| **U7** | **Under-scoped / incomplete fix (multi-file blindness)** — patch addresses one file when the issue implies several call-sites | SWE-bench performance collapses as gold-patch file count grows — **2310.06770, 2509.13941** | Partly covered by **L2 (consumer/blast-radius)** but L2 is rename-centric. Broaden L2 to "does the plan's file/edit set match the scope the issue implies?" via call-graph. | MEDIUM-HIGH |
| **U8** | **Premise-endorsement sycophancy** — plan uncritically builds on a flawed premise in the ticket that the codebase contradicts | Anthropic: preference models prefer sycophantic answer 95% of time; user-suggested wrong answer cuts accuracy up to 27% — **2310.13548** | Light addition to B4 (intent fidelity): flag plans that restate a ticket assumption and proceed with zero verification *where the codebase contradicts it*. (Absence of pushback ≠ sycophancy — keep it narrow.) | HIGH (behavior), but coding-specific manifestation is INFERRED/CONTESTED |
| **U9** | **Long-plan context degradation** — critical constraints buried mid-plan get dropped; reliability decays with token count | Lost-in-the-middle ~20pp swing (**2307.03172**); reasoning degrades far below window limit (**2402.14848, 2404.06654, 2502.05167**) | Cheap structural flag: plan/context **token count** + constraints-not-at-edges. Risk signal only, can't predict the dropped item. **L1 sizes the task, not the plan's own length.** | MEDIUM-HIGH (peer-reviewed backbone); "context rot" term is single vendor report |

**Modes that are NOT statically catchable** (state honestly — the gate's blind spots; pair with runtime guardrails, not more plan dims): plan-vs-execution drift and MAST's two largest modes — step repetition 15.7% + reasoning-action mismatch 13.2% (~29% combined, all runtime); multi-turn/challenge sycophancy (2502.08177 SycEval); whether the model would actually backtrack at runtime; whether mid-context info was in fact dropped; reward-hacking *acts* (vs intent in the plan); step-level fault attribution (unreliable even post-hoc — best 14.2%, **2505.00212**).

**Two cross-cutting caveats on the catalog:** (a) frequencies from PlanBench/TravelPlanner/Blocksworld are synthetic-domain and pre-o1 — the *modes persist* but *rates are dated* on frontier models (e.g. o1 jumps Mystery-Blocksworld from ~0% to ~52.8%); treat them as ranking, not calibration. (b) The brief's "CodeHalu = 2409.20550" is **wrong** — the actual ID is **2405.00253**; I could not verify any code-hallucination paper at 2409.20550.

---

## 2. Three-valued verdict (PASS / AMBIGUOUS / FAIL with abstain) — VERDICT: supported, with one binding condition

**Yes — an explicit "abstain when uncertain" verdict is a recognized, in places provably-correct pattern. But it lowers error only on the cases you still rule on, and only to the degree the abstain *trigger* correlates with correctness. The trigger design is the whole ballgame.**

- **Well-established theory (decades, with proofs):** Chow's reject rule (1970, IEEE IT); risk-coverage formalism (El-Yaniv & Wiener, JMLR 2010); provable error reduction on the accepted set (Geifman & El-Yaniv, **1705.08500**, NeurIPS 2017 — e.g. guaranteed 2% ImageNet top-5 error at ~60% coverage). Deep Gamblers (**1907.00208**) literally adds an abstain class — the direct analog of AMBIGUOUS.
- **Directly on-point, peer-reviewed:** **Trust or Escalate** (Jung/Brahman/Choi, ICLR 2025 Oral, **2407.18370**) — the citation to lead with. Cheapest judge first, abstain-and-escalate below a calibrated confidence threshold; distribution-free guarantee that accepted-set agreement meets target; 78-87% cheaper than always-GPT-4. Critically, it shows abstained items are *exactly* where humans disagree (κ 0.815 abstained vs 0.902 accepted) — so forcing a binary call there is precisely where a forced judge manufactures FP/FN. "Arbiters of Ambivalence" (**2505.23820**) independently shows judges over-decide on no-consensus inputs and neutralize more sensibly when a third option exists.

**The binding condition (most load-bearing finding):** the abstain trigger must be a **calibrated, error-ranking signal validated on labeled data — NOT the judge's raw/verbalized confidence** (AUROC ≈ 0.55, near-useless). Use multi-sample agreement / self-consistency (Trust-or-Escalate's Simulated Annotators), ensemble-agreement (robust to miscalibration), or a trained calibrator. Set the threshold on a labeled calibration set to a target error rate.

**When it just shifts the problem (flag these):** (a) miscalibrated/uncorrelated trigger → coverage collapse or relocated error; (b) the reviewer is no stronger than the planner → hard ceiling (Dorner/Nastl/Hardt NeurIPS 2024, **2410.13341**: no debiasing beats ~2× ground-truth labels); (c) abstention can magnify subgroup disparities (**2010.14134**); (d) if AMBIGUOUS routes to a human, abstention can *bias* that human (Jabbour 2025, **2508.07617**). The cost is always coverage loss.

**Sobriety flags:** Varshney et al. (ACL 2022, **2203.00211**) — across 17 datasets no selective-prediction method consistently beat plain MaxProb (the gap the conformal methods close *with a guarantee*). AbstentionBench (Meta FAIR 2025, **2506.09038**) — "abstention is an unsolved problem… scaling is of little use," and reasoning fine-tuning *degrades* it ~24%. Several 2025-2026 selective-judging citations (SCOPE, conformal-interval) are preprints, not yet peer-reviewed — directionally consistent but individually thin.

**Design implication:** keep PASS/AMBIGUOUS/FAIL, but make **AMBIGUOUS = abstain-and-escalate** (to a stronger judge or human), not a terminal answer. This aligns with the design doc's synthesis note #1, *with the correction* that the doc's "AMBIGUOUS → surface-but-overridable advisory" should be tightened to "AMBIGUOUS → escalate," and the trigger must not be raw confidence.

---

## 3. Verdict-composition A / B / C — VERDICT: (C), but built up from (A), NOT carved down from (B)

**The evidence most supports (C) — hybrid — implemented as "(A)-by-default with a *narrow, evidence-qualified* set of judgment dims promoted to block-with-override." (A) is the safe floor; (C) is its productive refinement; (B) is the option the evidence most directly argues against.**

**Important framing correction (I verified this against the source).** The design doc and the brief both gloss the Kambhampati ~84% as "false positives that block legitimate work" (over-rejection). The actual result in **2310.08118** (GPT-4 self-verifying 100 Blocksworld plans, ground truth = sound validator VAL) is the **opposite direction**: the LLM verifier wrongly **accepted 38/45 invalid plans as valid = 84.45%** — i.e. it *rubber-stamped bad plans*; its false-*rejection* of valid plans was only 1/55 = 1.8%. LLM+LLM self-critique scored 55% vs LLM+VAL (sound) 88% vs no-feedback 40%. The companion result (**2402.08115**, also verified) shows verifier error is *asymmetric and domain-dependent* — FP-dominated in Blocksworld (18.6%), FN-catastrophic in Mystery-Blocksworld (97%). **This strengthens the case against gating on LLM judgment in both directions:** an LLM gate is simultaneously too lenient (lets bad plans through) on some inputs and too strict on others, unpredictably. Treat "~84% FP" precisely as "84% false-*acceptance* on invalid Blocksworld plans," not generic over-blocking.

**(i) Empirical reliability of gating on LLM judgment — indefensible for hard correctness.** Beyond Kambhampati: intrinsic self-correction *degrades* reasoning (Huang et al., ICLR 2024, **2310.01798** — GSM8K 95.5→89.0, CommonSenseQA 75.8→41.8). The load-bearing distinction: LLM verifiers are decent at **ranking** (best-of-N: PRM 78.2% on MATH, **2305.20050**) but weak and **gameable** at **absolute accept/reject** (master-key tokens push judge FP to ~80%+, **2507.08794**; step-level gating near-random, ProcessBench **2412.06559**, PRMBench **2501.03124**). The oft-cited "~85% LLM-judge/human agreement" (**2306.05685**) is *preference on chat quality, not error detection* — the same paper shows naive math-grading failed 70% of the time. Do not import best-of-N optimism into a gate.

**(ii) LLM-Modulo (2402.01817, verified) prescribes exactly the (A)/(C) partition:** LLM = candidate *generator*; **hard/correctness critics MUST be sound/model-based** (VAL, model checkers, unit tests, simulators); **soft/style critics MAY be LLM-based** but never own the accept decision — "the soundness of the framework is inherited from the soundness of the hard critics." This is the published architecture closest to our gate.

**(iii) Override/escalation & the FP-disable threshold — the decisive argument against (B):** The static-analysis literature is consistent and quantitative. Coverity (Bessey et al., CACM 2010): **>30% FP → "people ignore the tool"**, target **<20%**, and 3 bad first reports → "this tool sucks." Google Tricorder (Sadowski et al., CACM 2018): enable only **<10% "effective FP"** (= any report the dev doesn't act on — behavioral, not technical), build-breakers need **~0%**, ≥25% not-useful → turned off. Microsoft (Christakis & Bird, ASE 2016): only **24% of devs tolerate 20% FP**; 90% want ≤5%. Facebook (CACM 2019): *surfacing timing* matters more than precision — same analysis at diff/review time hit >70% fix rate vs near-zero in batch. An LLM-judgment gate at the measured ~84% (or even charitable double-digit) FP rate is **8-80× past the abandonment threshold** — it would be disabled, rubber-stamped, or routed around, taking the trustworthy mechanical checks with it. (B)'s "block-by-default, override-with-justification" makes the override the *normal* path, which is indistinguishable from no gate but worse for trust.

**Why (C)-from-(A), with two hard constraints:**
1. The blocking set must be **sound, not merely named** — a judgment dim earns the block-with-override tier *only if* it's tool-checkable OR empirically demonstrated to clear ~10% effective-FP on your own traffic. Naming a free-form judgment dim as blocking just reconstructs (B) for that dim. In practice this collapses most hard-blockers onto the mechanical/tool checks (B0, the sound parts of B1/L2/L4) — which is exactly the design doc's settled "block on sound/mechanical, advise on LLM judgment."
2. The override must be the **trust-preserving kind**: rare-by-design, audited, justification-required (cf. GCP Binary Authorization breakglass → auto-logged), regression-only baselining (block on *new* findings), and accountable feedback (Google's "not useful" → bug against the check). This matches the design doc's "fail-CLOSED, recorded override with justification" — the evidence endorses it *provided the blocking tier is near-zero-FP*.

Make advisory findings **generative, not silent logs** — LLM-Modulo's "advisory" critic *back-prompts a retry*, which is where the value is; a purely-logged finding has no teeth (the design doc's "durably recorded, overridable" should bias toward "feeds a revise-the-plan loop").

**Net:** the design doc's settled position ("block on sound/mechanical; advise on LLM judgment") is **(A)**, and is well-supported as the floor. The refinement to **(C)** is justified *only* for judgment dims you have measured to be reliable. Do not adopt (B).

**Contested points kept honest:** the Kambhampati pessimism is partly self-citing (2 of 3 pessimist papers are the same ASU group, GPT-4-era, few domains) and the strong "LLMs can't plan" claim is polemical/contested — but the *conditional* reading holds for exactly the hard-correctness dims you'd want to block (Song et al. "Mind the Gap" **2412.02674**: verification beats generation *except* on tasks beyond the model's own competence — the planning regime). Multi-agent debate as a rescue is disputed (Du **2305.14325** vs Huang's "disguised self-consistency" rebuttal) — don't lean on it.

---

## 4. Cost / shift-left economics of a per-pickup plan gate

**(1) Is shift-left economically justified? Directionally defensible, quantitatively folklore — flag the cost curve explicitly.**
- A wrong *plan* is a design/requirements-level defect, the class shift-left most plausibly helps — so the *direction* is sound.
- BUT the precise multipliers ("1:10:100", "10x-100x per phase", "30x") are **poorly sourced / contested**: Boehm's 1981 underlying data was never published; Boehm & Basili's "100x" (IEEE Computer 2001) carries a *stripped* caveat that small systems are "more like 5:1"; the "IBM Systems Sciences Institute" chart traces to unlocatable training notes; NIST 2002's "30x" table is **explicitly labelled "(Example Only)"** with no source. Bossavit's *Leprechauns of Software Engineering* documents the circular citation chain; **Menzies et al. (2016, 1609.04886)** re-tested on 171 projects and found **"no evidence for the delayed issue effect"** — the effect flattens in iterative/CI workflows. **Do not justify the gate on the phase-cost curve.**

**(2) The cheap-prefilter → selective-LLM → model-routing pattern — well-grounded, with quantified savings.**
- Theory: selective prediction (Chow 1970; Geifman & El-Yaniv **1705.08500**) — the structural prefilter is the selection function `g`, the LLM is the expensive `f` invoked only on the uncertain residual; concentrating spend on the low-confidence region is provably efficient. Canonical cheap-first cascade: Viola-Jones (~15× speedup by killing obvious cases in a 2-feature first stage).
- LLM cascades at *fixed quality*: FrugalGPT (**2305.05176**) up to ~98% cost cut (best case, classification-shaped, 2023 prices); RouteLLM (**2406.18665**) >85% cost cut at 95% GPT-4 quality on MT-Bench; AutoMix (**2310.12963**) >50% via self-verify-then-escalate. **Robust band: 50-85% at matched quality**; ~98% is best-case.
- **Generalization caveat:** RouterBench (**2403.12031**) — across 405k outcomes, no router significantly beats no-routing *overall* (wins on some task types, loses on others); cascade degrades fast once judge error >0.2. Treat headline % as upper bounds. Naive "defer if logprob < τ" is undercut by Jitkrittum et al. (NeurIPS 2023, **2307.02764**: post-hoc deferral beats confidence-deferral under shift) and Gupta et al. (**2404.10136**: sequence-logprob is length-biased).

**(3) Break-even framing — when the gate is worth running.** Run the gate when:
> P(real plan flaw) × P(gate catches it) × cost-if-escaped > cost-of-running-gate + P(false alarm) × cost-of-false-alarm

It **loses** when the plan-flaw base rate is low (screening math: at low prevalence even an accurate test is mostly false alarms), the escape cost is modest (the folklore caveat — escalation is often flat), or false-alarm cost is high (wasted re-plan cycles + latency on every pickup). The dominant lever: **condition the gate on uncertainty/difficulty, don't fire it flat** — Snell et al. (**2408.03314**): difficulty-conditioned compute is >4× more efficient than flat; Gao et al. (**2210.10760**): optimizing against an imperfect verifier makes *true* quality peak-then-decline (Goodhart).

**(4) Concrete cost-scoping guidance:**
- Architect as **deterministic structural prefilter (≈free — the existing B0/clarity/check_ac heuristics) → Haiku-class cheap LLM on the residual → escalate to Opus-class only on low confidence.** An 80%-resolving prefilter cuts the LLM bill and latency ~5×.
- Per-call review cost ≈ $0.005 (Haiku 4.5) / $0.013 (Sonnet) / $0.025 (Opus) for a ~3k-in/400-out judge turn; multiply by the **residual fraction, not total ticket volume**. Prompt caching (~0.1× input on cache reads) and Batch API (50% off, for non-latency-sensitive runs) further reduce.
- **Ground the verifier in sound external signal** (the codebase, test/type-check/verify-command outputs, file-impact data) — not introspection — because LLM self-verification on planning is net-negative (2310.01798, 2402.08115). This is the same conclusion as thread 3 (route hard properties to sound checks).
- **Run it async / off the critical path.** Even Haiku brushes the 1s "flow" threshold (~4-7s for a verdict); Opus crosses the 10s "lose attention" line. A synchronous gate on every pickup is a forced-serial Amdahl bottleneck across parallel agents. Strong option that fits the design doc's architecture: do the heavy review **out-of-band**, and make `claim` a fast check for a valid signed attestation (which the doc already specifies) — so the latency tax lands once, async, not on every pickup.
- **Citation hygiene:** the "23 minutes to recover from an interruption" figure is *not* from Gloria Mark's peer-reviewed work — don't cite it. The "context rot" term is a single vendor report (mild RAG-selling incentive) — the peer-reviewed backbone (2402.14848, 2404.06654, 2502.05167) is what to cite.

---

### Bottom-line deltas to the current design

1. **Three-valued verdict: keep it**, but tighten AMBIGUOUS from "advisory/overridable" to **"abstain-and-escalate,"** and never trigger abstention on raw judge confidence — use multi-sample agreement / a calibrated trigger validated on labeled data.
2. **Composition: adopt (C)-from-(A), reject (B).** Block only on sound/tool checks; promote a judgment dim to block-with-override *only* once measured under ~10% effective-FP on your own traffic; keep overrides rare/audited/regression-baselined; make advisory findings generative (feed a revise loop), not silent logs.
3. **Correct the Kambhampati framing in the catalog/synthesis** — the ~84% is false-*acceptance* of invalid plans (rubber-stamping), and verifier error is asymmetric by domain. Both directions argue against gating on LLM judgment.
4. **Add 9 uncovered checks** (U1-U9 above), prioritizing U1 (package/registry existence — extend B1), U2 (fault-localization), U5 (missing-verification/termination), U4 (irreversible-action sniff — make O4's destructive check always-on), and U6 (self-review-as-verification).
5. **Cost: don't justify on the phase-cost curve; justify on base-rate × escape-cost, condition on difficulty, prefilter→cheap→escalate, ground in sound signal, run async.**

All four threads' load-bearing primary claims (MAST 14-mode breakdown, Plan-and-Solve 27% semantic-misunderstanding persistence, TravelPlanner 0.6%, LLM-Modulo partition, Kambhampati 84% false-acceptance, self-verification collapse) were independently verified against the source papers, not taken from subagents alone.
